• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Mice

Tumor cells trajectory using TInGa

Audrey

2023-04-25

This script is used to infer trajectory on tumor cells, using TInGa.

library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

.libPaths()
## [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/library"

Preparation

In this section, we set the global settings of the analysis. We will store data there :

save_name = "mice_tumor_cells_subset"
out_dir = "."

We load the sample information :

sample_info = readRDS(file = paste0(out_dir, "/../1_metadata/mice_sample_info.rds"))
project_names_oi = as.character(sample_info$project_name)

graphics::pie(rep(1, nrow(sample_info)),
              col = sample_info$color,
              labels = sample_info$project_name)

This are the settings for trajectory inference :

traj_dimred = "mnn"          # to infer trajectory on
traj_umap = "mnn_dm_umap"    # just of visualization
seed = 1337L
traj_max_dims = 41           # to infer trajectory on

We load the Seurat object :

sobj = readRDS(paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_sobj.rds"))
sobj
## An object of class Seurat 
## 21564 features across 45573 samples within 2 assays 
## Active assay: RNA (19564 features, 2000 variable features)
##  1 other assay present: mnn.reconstructed
##  8 dimensional reductions calculated: RNA_pca, RNA_pca_41_tsne, RNA_pca_41_umap, mnn, mnn_41_tsne, mnn_41_umap, mnn_dm, mnn_dm_umap

Trajectory inference

We set the root manually :

root_cell_id = "2020_09_AGAAATGCAACCGATT-1"
sobj$is_root = colnames(sobj) == root_cell_id
sobj$cell_name = colnames(sobj)
root_plot = aquarius::plot_label_dimplot(sobj, reduction = traj_umap,
                                         col_by = "is_root", col_color = c("gray80", "red"),
                                         label_by = "cell_name", label_val = root_cell_id) +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Root cell") +
  ggplot2::theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
                 aspect.ratio = 1) +
  Seurat::NoLegend() + Seurat::NoAxes()

sobj$is_root = NULL
sobj$cell_name = NULL

root_plot

Now, we perform the trajectory inference :

set.seed(seed)
my_traj = aquarius::traj_tinga(sobj,
                               seed = seed,
                               expression_assay = "RNA",
                               expression_slot = "data",
                               count_assay = "RNA",
                               count_slot = "counts",
                               dimred_name = traj_dimred,
                               dimred_max_dim = traj_max_dims,
                               root_cell_id = root_cell_id,
                               tinga_parameters = list(max_nodes = 8))

## Add pseudotime
sobj$pseudotime = my_traj$pseudotime

(Time to run : 32.11 s)

Visualization

We visualize pseudotime :

Seurat::FeaturePlot(sobj, features = "pseudotime",
                    reduction = traj_umap,
                    cols = viridis::viridis(n = 100)) +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Pseudotime") +
  ggplot2::theme(aspect.ratio = 1,
                 plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
  Seurat::NoAxes()

We visualize the trajectory with arrows :

dynplot::plot_dimred(trajectory = my_traj,
                     label_milestones = TRUE,
                     plot_trajectory = TRUE,
                     dimred = sobj[[traj_umap]]@cell.embeddings,
                     color_cells = 'pseudotime', color_trajectory = "none")

Save

We save the Seurat object :

saveRDS(sobj, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_sobj_traj_tinga.rds"))
